hypothesis: The overnight loop becomes more deterministic if runner-stability ratchets must target exactly one recovery, queue-invalidation, or stale-cycle failure surface and prove a closed-fail rule with replayable evidence commands.
failure_mode: A runner hardening card can currently span recovery, queue invalidation, and stale-cycle handling at once, so stale-cycle safety improvements remain under-specified and cannot be falsified lane-by-lane.
closed_fail_rule: Keep the slice only if runner-stability cards fail verification unless they bind to one explicit failure surface and declare replayable runner evidence commands.
measurement: Before the ratchet, `sysopt.runner-stability-ratchet` could span recovery, queue invalidation, and stale-cycle handling in one vague card with no explicit failure boundary or replayable evidence commands. After the ratchet, `node CLAW/scripts/verify-systems-optimizer.mjs` rejects any `sysopt.runner-stability-*` card that omits a singular `runner_focus`, `failure_mode`, `closed_fail_rule`, or runner evidence commands, and the retained card is narrowed to `stale-cycle` while the scope-local promotion bundle stays green.
keep_rule: Keep only if the verifier rejects vague runner-stability cards lacking one explicit focus plus evidence commands and the fixed scope-local evaluation bundle passes without touching route implementation paths.
learning_item: Runner-stability ratchets must reduce to one named failure surface with an explicit closed-fail rule and replayable evidence commands; multi-surface runner hardening language is too vague to promote.
